mission.title: Install and Configure vLLM
mission.description: vLLM is a high-throughput and memory-efficient inference and serving engine for large language models (LLMs). It is ideal for applications requiring fast and scalable LLM serving, leveraging advanced techniques for memory management and request batching. Key trade-offs include the need for specific hardware optimizations and configurations to achieve optimal performance.

steps.title: Install the CLI
steps.description:
This step installs the vLLM command line interface to interact with the vLLM service.

```bash

pip install vllm==0.19.0

```

> Install the specific version of vLLM.

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
This command creates a new Kubernetes cluster with the specified version and node count in the desired region.

```bash

gcloud container clusters create vllm-cluster --cluster-version=1.31 --num-nodes=3 --region=us-central1

```

> Adjust region and node count as necessary.

steps.title: Set Up kubeconfig
steps.description:
This command configures your local kubeconfig to connect to the newly created cluster.

```bash

gcloud container clusters get-credentials vllm-cluster --region=us-central1

```

> Ensure you have the correct permissions.

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the nodes in the cluster to ensure they are ready.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> You should see all nodes in 'Ready' status.

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
This step sets up autoscaling and monitoring for the vLLM deployment.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/vllm-project/vllm/main/deploy/autoscaler.yaml

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/vllm-project/vllm/main/deploy/monitoring.yaml

```

> This configures the Horizontal Pod Autoscaler.
> This sets up monitoring for the deployment.

uninstall.title: Uninstall vLLM
uninstall.description:
This command removes the vLLM deployment and associated resources. Be aware that this may lead to data loss and orphaned resources.

```bash

kubectl delete deployment vllm-deployment

gcloud container clusters delete vllm-cluster --region=us-central1

```

> This removes the vLLM deployment.
> This deletes the entire cluster and associated resources.

upgrade.title: Upgrade vLLM
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up your data and configurations. This step will upgrade vLLM to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl drain <node-name> --ignore-daemonsets

kubectl set image deployment/vllm-deployment vllm-container=vllm:0.19.0

kubectl uncordon <node-name>

kubectl rollout status deployment/vllm-deployment

```

> Replace <node-name> with the actual node name.
> This updates the vLLM container image.
> This allows scheduling on the node again.
> Verify that the deployment is updated successfully.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'ImagePullBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The specified image version may not exist or is incorrect.

**Fix:**
Check the image name and tag in the deployment configuration.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'CrashLoopBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The application may be failing to start due to misconfiguration.

**Fix:**
Check the logs using 'kubectl logs <pod-name>' for details.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient CPU'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The cluster may not have enough resources allocated for the deployment.

**Fix:**
Increase the node size or number of nodes in the cluster.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'FailedScheduling'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** No nodes are available that match the resource requests.

**Fix:**
Adjust the resource requests in the deployment configuration.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new quantization techniques and improved memory management features.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: Update your deployment configurations to use the new quantization methods.

resolution.summary: You should see output similar to 'NAME STATUS ROLES AGE VERSION' with all nodes in 'Ready' state when running 'kubectl get nodes'.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the necessary tools and cloud permissions to create and manage Kubernetes clusters.
